_meta.purpose: Reward-director expert baselines for by-level matrix metrics with no free UK source
_meta.discipline: expert estimate, NOT survey anchor; gradient renders, market-position verdict suppressed unless overridden

pension_by_level.floor_note: Supervisor + Frontline = 3% statutory employer minimum (auto-enrolment, qualifying earnings)
pension_by_level.verdict_note: expert baseline - gradient renders, LOWER/HIGHER THAN MARKET + P1-P3 suppressed until real peer data backs it (default; David may override)

car_allowance_by_level.shape: tapering ladder from top, cliffs out at Manager (senior-and-above benefit)

Manager.note: £4k WHERE offered, but few employers offer at this level - render as rare, not a norm

Supervisor / Team Leader.note: not offered - N/A, off the benefit, NOT £0

Frontline / Individual Contributor.note: not offered - N/A, off the benefit, NOT £0

car_allowance_by_level.verdict_note: expert baseline - gradient renders where offered; not-offered levels excluded from benchmark entirely (N/A not £0); P-position suppressed (default)

bonus_by_level.quantity: target bonus as % of base salary
bonus_by_level.shape: steep ladder (40:1 top-to-bottom) WHERE bonuses exist, but existence is SECTOR-GATED

SECTOR_GATE.critical: Bonus EXISTENCE is sector-gated, not just level-gated. The 40:1 ladder is the value curve WHERE bonuses are paid.
SECTOR_GATE.no_bonus_note: These sectors give NO bonus at ANY level - must render 'no bonus scheme', NOT the ladder. Storing the ladder flat would misrepresent every charity/public org.
SECTOR_GATE.wiring_required: Engine must sector-gate this metric: charity/public -> no-bonus; bonus-paying sectors -> the ladder. If engine cannot sector-gate, metric carries heavy 'sector-dependent, many sectors nil' caveat instead of a clean ladder.

bonus_by_level.verdict_note: expert baseline - ladder renders in bonus-paying sectors only; P-position suppressed

annual_leave_by_level.quantity: annual leave entitlement (days, excluding bank holidays)
annual_leave_by_level.shape: FLAT across seniority levels - does NOT ladder by level

Board / Executive.note: flat - same at every level (standard mid-market)

Director.note: flat - same at every level (standard mid-market)

Head of.note: flat - same at every level (standard mid-market)

Senior Manager.note: flat - same at every level (standard mid-market)

Manager.note: flat - same at every level (standard mid-market)

Supervisor / Team Leader.note: flat - same at every level (standard mid-market)

Frontline / Individual Contributor.note: flat - same at every level (standard mid-market)

SECTOR_BAND.critical: Value is SECTOR-driven, not level-driven. Level axis is flat; sector sets the number.

statutory_floor_sectors.note: 20 days + 8 BH = 28 total = UK statutory minimum (5.6 weeks)

TENURE_EXCEPTION.note: In these sectors leave rises with YEARS OF SERVICE (tenure), not seniority level. A by-LEVEL chart is the wrong axis here - needs a tenure view or 'varies by service' caveat, not the flat number.

annual_leave_by_level.CHART_AXIS_FLAG: This metric does NOT vary by seniority level. Render FLAT (states 'leave does not ladder' - true & useful) rather than a synthetic Board>Frontline gradient. The engine's current seniority gradient on annual leave is WRONG in shape, not just altitude.

lti_eligibility_by_level.quantity: % eligible for long-term incentives, by level
lti_eligibility_by_level.shape: CLIFF at Director (exec + director tier eligible, nothing below) - NOT a smooth ladder

CHART_CORRECTION.current_wrong: Image 1 renders 38/26/11/4/1/1/0 - a smooth descending ladder
CHART_CORRECTION.problem_1: UNDERSTATES exec/director badly (38 vs ~90, 26 vs 70) - shared descending ladder lands too low at top
CHART_CORRECTION.problem_2: WRONG SHAPE - shows a smooth tail (Head-of 11, SM 4, Mgr 1); real LTI is a CLIFF at Director, ~0 below
CHART_CORRECTION.fix: replace smooth ladder with exec+director cliff; zero below Director

SECTOR_GATE.critical: LTI EXISTENCE is sector-gated, like bonus. The cliff is the shape WHERE LTI is run.
SECTOR_GATE.note: These sectors run NO LTI at any level. Cliff renders only in LTI-running sectors (finance, tech, listed, large corporate).

lti_eligibility_by_level.normalisation_bug: SEPARATE: Image 1 Director row renders 26%/75%=101% - rounding/normalisation defect, fix in matrix code regardless of baseline
